codeReview.summary: The fix does add explicit VAL-EXEC-001 failed/cancelled packet-level assertions and removes the prior result-only blind spot, but the new sync predicate now rewrites non-terminal packets on every transport cycle. This introduces continuous mission snapshot churn unrelated to state changes and is a blocking regression in syncMissionExecution.

issues.description: The condition `if isTerminalPhase || desiredPacketStatus != packet.status || packet.completedAt == nil` is always true for non-terminal packets (their `completedAt` is normally nil), so `syncMissionExecution` persists `mission_packet_synced` updates every cycle even when run phase and packet status are unchanged. This causes unnecessary state/event churn and `updatedAt` drift for in-progress missions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-orchestration skill still documents outdated XCTest filter names (`OpenBurnBar*`) that diverge from canonical service commands (`BurnBar*`), increasing risk of false-green runs with zero matched tests.

summary: Review failed. The rerun correctly adds explicit non-skipped VAL-EXEC-001 failed/cancelled packet assertions, but commit 7cca143d introduces a blocking sync predicate regression that rewrites non-terminal packets every cycle.
